entry.title: Use of authorization group in tile Manage Posting Periods not working for specific journal entries
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

We want to limit employees of our organization to post in previous periods for certain general ledger accounts. For this we are using special periods and an authorization group.

In this example we want to avoid that employees can post to general ledger account DIGITS in a previous period. In the tile Manage Posting Periods we have made set up Adjustment Periods for general ledger account DIGITS with an authorization group. And we have given only a few employees access rights to post in the adjustment periods.

However, what we see if an employee creates a CO posting in app Reassign Costs and Revenues in one of the adjustment periods and has no authorization for the authorization group assigned, that this employee does not get an authorization message that he/she is not allowed to post in an adjustment period. We would expect that also at a CO posting an authorization message comes up, as also a financial document will be created in general ledger.

Why?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Use of authorization group in tile Manage Posting Per...
entry.content:
Review kba 2598676 - Manage Posting Periods Authorization

in full.

See referenced therein the following stated 

..

  • If no Authorization Group is specified for user, for example Write Access Unrestricted for role with object Authorization Group for Posting in Adjustment Periods, user can post in both periods 1 and 2

  • If a user is assigned two roles, one for which Write access is restricted and Authorization Group for Posting in adjustment Periods is blank or <Not Maintained>, as well as a second role for which there’s no Write restrictions for Authorization Group for Posting in adjustment Periods, then the user can post in both Periods 1 and 2.

  • If a user is assigned two roles, one for which Write access is restricted and Authorization Group for Posting in adjustment Periods is blank or <Not Maintained> as well as a second role for which there’s Write restrictions for Authorization Group for Posting in adjustment Periods, then the user can post in both Periods 1 and 2.

entry.title: RAP Service Unmanaged Save with CDS Parameters
entry.content:
We want to create a RAP Service using a CDS View which relies on Parameters.

 

define root view entity ZTEST_CO_VIEW
  with parameters
    p_fiscalYear        : gjahr
  as select from ZTEST_CI_VIEW(  p_fiscalYear : $parameters.p_fiscalYear )
{       
    key matnr,
    field1,
    field2
}

 

However, when creating a behavior for this View and exposing as a Service (we would like to use unmanaged Save), we get the following error message:

 

Non-hidden view parameters are not allowed in transactional entity ZTEST_CO_VIEW

 

Am I right to understand that I cannot use CDS View Parameters with Behaviors?

Does someone know if this is documented somewhere?

Thanks and regards,

Axel

entry.title: Re: SAP Companion Usage and Context Help Visits reports a...
entry.content: What Shane said, but with one clarification. The parameter specifies the Workarea in which you want the tracking information to be stored - not the Workarea containing the help content. This allows you to consolidate tracking data across systems, or separate it out, per system. Personally I recommend keeping it separate (and set to the same Workarea as the help content is delivered from - which is why it is often confused) as for most of the reports you can choose multiple Workareas anyway.

entry.title: Re: BOBJ DataProviderSQL Occasionally Shows "#SECURITY"
entry.content: Verify if the user account used for scheduled instances has the same access permissions as the account used for refreshing the report.

entry.title: Re: SD self-billing - SAFT
entry.content:
Hello,

First, thank you for the answer and help.

We are currently analyzing Chapter 10.2.2. When we try to classify this document as an external document, we are required to migrate certain original information into SAP, such as the document date, document number, and digital signature. However, in our case, we do not have this information. For instance, the end customer may not have a digital signature, especially in situations where the end customer is not a Portuguese.
We believe the "external document" classification only applies if the document is part of software owned by another company in the group, which is not the case for us.

Best regards,

Ana

entry.title: Start Your scripting Journey The Easy Way With SAP Analytics Cloud | Part Four
entry.content:
The Journey

I’ve been using SAP Analytics Cloud for almost 8 years and like many of you, have stuck to Stories rather than Analytic Applications as I don’t have a massive coding background. Well if truth be told, I’m old and so my Basic and Pascal skills haven’t had an airing for quite a few years.

With the latest innovations such as the Optimised Design Experience and the ability to add script elements to existing stories, I have now started to ‘enhance’ my visual storytelling. It struck me that rather than go big bang I would take an existing SAC Story and add code where I could get some quick wins without having to write big chunks of code.

Where to start this journey - that was the question and this piece continues from Part Three where I show small code elements that help me to create a more appealing, usable story that adds a little more finesse to the standard story interface. Now in my 4th part of the series we look at a couple more simple scripting elements to help you make your stories more engaging.

But before we look at a couple more small chunks of code to enhance your stories, I thought it would be good to share some resources that I've found invaluable on this journey. Let's start with some free education in the basics of scripting within SAP Analytics Cloud. I found a very nice course on learning.sap.com that give you the basics of scripting within the new Optimised Story environment.

You can find the link to the course below but for guided learning I found this to be a most excellent resource.

  • Acquiring Basic Scripting Skills to Extend Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud

Once I started this course there were certain concepts I was still struggling with because I don't have a scripting background - now I was stuck !! The solution was to learn the basics of JavaScript as well as the specific application within SAC. So, what to do? Well, the answer was pretty simple as there are a host of excellent resources available to teach yourself JavaScript.

The one I settled on (in no way affiliated to SAP) was a course entitled "JavaScript - Perfect for Beginners" from the code-basis.com website. Links to the specific course are here:

  • JavaScript - Perfect for Beginners

So that's a couple of very useful training courses I've now taken but there are a few more links that I think are worth sharing giving some help on the method and functions available within the SAC environment:

  • Scripting (Optimized Story Experience and Analytics Designer)
  • Optimized Story Experience API Reference Guide
  • Analytics Designer API Reference Guide

One more link that I've found useful explains the various conventions in object naming and as you'll come to see everything now needs a name - pages, objects, scripts, functions, you name it... and you'll need to name it. 

  • Scripting Naming Conventions 

So, what little scripting chunk are we going to look at in this instalment? Well today I thought we'd start with a quick Measure Swap on a simple chart - Like a Measure Input control but done via scripting to show the theory behind reusing a chart but changing a specific element via the coding API.

So if you've used SAC for any length of time you'll have seen that you can create a Measure Input Control or a Dimension Input Control in a story. These controls allow a user to swap measures or dimensions in a chart by simply selecting the desired items from a list added to the story.

This does not change with the new Optimised Story option, but I thought it would be a good introduction to changing chart elements using scripting. Also, once you've understood changing Measures or Dimensions via scripting other chart elements follow similar patterns.

So, imagine for the sake of this example we have a story, and that story contains a chart. The chart's data model has 3 different dimensions and 3 different measures.

Measures:

  • Total Revenue (TR)
  • Total Costs (TC)
  • Units

Dimensions:

  • Categories
  • Countries
  • Brands

Charts:

  • Chart_1

For this example, we will create the following story with a column chart containing 1 measure and 1 dimension. Then we'll add a couple of buttons to swap measures and swap dimensions so you can see the steps required. First the video showing the completed exercise - the chart on the left is the one we'll use for the scripting, the chart on the right uses Input Controls to swap those items around.

So, as you can see from the above illustration you can control what Dimension or Measure is displayed in a chart at the push of a button. But what is in that script exactly - well the logic is pretty simple:

 

 

 

// These are the steps and not the actual code

Work out what is the current measure in the chart
Remove the existing Measure from the Chart
Add the new Measure to the Chart

 

 

In practice you can use the Ctrl-Spacebar hint to 'show' you the options as you start to write the code but let's look at the actual code behind the 1st button:

 

 

// Remove the current Measure from the chart and add the new Measure

// Create a variable to hold the current chart measure using the getMembers() function
var TheCurrentMeasure = Chart_1.getMembers(Feed.ValueAxis);
console.log(TheCurrentMeasure[0]);

// Remove that measure so that the chart has no measures added to it
Chart_1.removeMember(Feed.ValueAxis,TheCurrentMeasure[0]);

// Add the desired measure to the chart to replace the measure removed above
Chart_1.addMember(Feed.ValueAxis,"[Account].[parentId].&[TR]");

 

 

So, what is each line of the code doing? Well as you should know by now SAC comments are preceded with the // characters so lines #1 and #3 are comments. Line #4 creates a variable called TheCurrentMeasure and is assigned the value by using the getMembers() function and the Feed.ValueAxis enumeration.

Put simply it gives you a list of the measures applied to the chart. Now the function actually returns an array with a member per measure but here we only have one measure in the chart. That means that we only have 1 element in the array. You can see the array values by looking at the browser console and in this case I print them out in Line #5.

We've created a variable, populated it with the existing measure applied to the chart and now we wish to remove that measure before we add the new one. Line #8 uses the removeMember() function to remove the specified Feed Element - in this case the ValueAxis and we remove it with the 1st element of the array. 

Chart_1.removeMember(Feed.ValueAxis,TheCurrentMeasure[0]);

Note: we use TheCurrentMeasure[0] to signify the 1st element of the array - remember the array contains all the measures that were applied to the chart so could contain more than 1. You can use a Loop construct to loop through all the members if there are more than one but here, we just use element [0].

OK almost done, we've removed the measure from the chart and so the final piece is to add a new measure it its place. For this we use the addMember() function to add a measure called [TR]:

Chart_1.addMember(Feed.ValueAxis,"[Account].[parentId].&[TR]");

That's it, we find the measure, remove the measure, and then add the measure that we wish to put in its place. It's the same as a Measure Input Control but shows the capabilities and flexibility of the Scripting API. We can of course do exactly the same thing with Dimensions with the following code:

 

 

// Create a variable to hold the current Dimension applied to the chart

var TheCurrentDimension = Chart_1.getDimensions(Feed.CategoryAxis);
console.log(TheCurrentDimension[0]);

// Remove that Dimension from the chart - it's array element [0]
Chart_1.removeDimension(TheCurrentDimension[0],Feed.CategoryAxis);

// Add the Channels dimension to the chart
Chart_1.addDimension("Channels",Feed.CategoryAxis);

 

 

Very similar in design to the example for measures but using the getDimensions(), removeDimension() and addDimension() calls to facilitate the code.

Some of you may have noticed that even with the scripting example the titles of the charts change as you change the Dimension or the Measure. Currently there is no scripting API to alter the chart title (don't get me started) but you can still get the title to change dynamically.

You simply add dynamic text to the chart title - in this case you can add Accounts & Dimensions into the chart title using the Add >> Dynamic Text and away you go.

Format appropriately and every time you change the measure or dimension with the script API the titles will change as well.

Happy Coding and before I go, here are the links to the previous episodes:

  • Part One
  • Part Two
  • Part Three

Enjoy and see you in Part Five due out before the end of the year. As always comment if you need clarification and I'll try to respond as soon as I can.

Daniel.

entry.title: Re: Skeleton System - Redeploy
entry.content:
@LucasBeraldi To request system rebuild you must use the "Additional Service" BASIC_1.8.25 described in the Roles and responsibilities document, unde the section "System Installation".

You must quote a new migration server and required temporary files storage.

Regards Comitre

entry.title: Re: Convert multiple Rows in a Single row with different columns in ALV Report
entry.content:
If you can select multiple years, yes dynamic, but if only one year you can define a static version with 12 or 16 amounts columns (1-12, or more depending on Financial calendar 1-16?)

In first case

  • Use @Sandra_Rossi 's answer with ASSIGN COMPONENT statement 

Else

  • identify the available periods (e.g. 2024-01, 2024-02, etc.) in the first internal table
  • And create a dynamic internal table - Create Dynamic Table using RTTS and display in ALV
  • Also use @Sandra_Rossi 's answer with ASSIGN COMPONENT statement

entry.title: GRV RECIPIENT NAME PRINT
entry.content:
We are encountering a challenge with our current ordering process in SAP S4 HANA. Specifically, when orders are placed for foremen, the Goods Receipt Voucher (GRV) only displays the name of the person who created the Purchase Requisition (PR), not the intended recipient. This causes delays as we have to manually check SAP to identify who the materials were ordered for, which is time-consuming. This is especially challenging as MCOs often order in bulk for multiple foremen in their areas of responsibility.

Is there a way to adjust the SAP process so that the GRV automatically reflects the recipient's name (e.g., Oupa)? Alternatively, could a report be generated that shows the intended recipient for the goods to streamline the collection process?

entry.title: Re: The new SAP Digital Manufacturing for Insights, including MDOs, Operational Reports and Embedded
entry.content:
Hi , I'm looking in SAP Documentation for information on Data Retention on the MDOs of SAP DM but I cannot find it unfortunately. 

Do you know or can help me with a contact on:

  • How long does DM store the MDO attributes generally or within each MDO for.
  • Is this time different between different MDOs? For example Data Collection Attributes are stored for a different period of times as Downtime MDO Parameters.
  • For how long can the individual MDO attributes be retrieved (if the retention/storage period in SAP DM is MDO attribute-specific)?

    Thanks for any infos or contacts! 

    RG




@ManoelCosta

entry.title: Re: No RFC authorization for function module RFCPING.
entry.content:
The new error is:

ERROR registration of tp RFC_MSAFDFE from host 10.xx.xxx.xx.. not allowed

This means the Gateway is not yet configured to allow your program to register. You need to enter a line like

P TP=RFC_MSAFDFE HOST=10.xx.xxx.xx ACCESS=<list of application servers that should be allowed to call your JCo program>

in the Gateway's "reginfo" file.

This can be done in transaction SMGW, usually under "Goto --> Expert Functions --> External Security". (The precise path depends a bit on the backend release.)

See RFC Gateway Security Files secinfo and reginfo | SAP Help Portal for details.

entry.title: CT-e solution based on TM Consignment Orders
entry.content:
Hello TM experts,

One of the main challenges of TM projects carried out in Brazil is to adapt freight calculations to the reality of CT-e entries, the carriers' tax collection document. All the solutions presented so far are unable to fully cover the needs of calculating and distributing costs in a linear planning model. Let me explain:

The CT-e is a tax document that is based on the origin and destination of the goods to charge and NF-es. Therefore, if I have 3 deliveries/NF-e departing from plant XXXX and arrives in different locations, three CT-es will be generated by carrier, each with the plant as the origin and the delivery location as the destination. In this way, we use the calculation by stage in the documents. And that's where the problems begin.

As I said, we need the origin and destination of the goods to calculate the CT-e, but when performing the calculation by stage, and using a calculation base that searches for data from the source location (like city location code, transportation zone) ​this data ​are searched from the source location of the stage and not from the load point location. Also for quantities "calculation bases" like weight, volume or goods value, everything that is present in that stage is taken into consideration, and not only what will be delivered, which makes sense for calculating the CT-e.

The same occurs when calculating the cost distribution, performing the prorating based on what is in the resource in the stage and not only what will be delivered. Below is a diagram that demonstrates this problem:

There are some solutions that avoid one or another custom development, but I haven't seen any that meet all the necessary requirements.

In order to mitigate the need for all these developments and continue using linear planning in freight orders, I created a new solution based on consignment order documents. These documents are logical groupings and when created from an FO, they have the exact parameters to calculate charges and cost distribution. Origin, destination, weight, volume and items that should be grouped in the same CT-e are grouped correctly without the need for developments.

It is possible to determine the correct time for automatic generation of the CO through the consignment building profile (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e3dc5400c1cc41d1bc0ae0e7fd9aa5a2/5985ebe395b64038814e14384e5a8c83.html). I created a basic prototype to demonstrate how it is possible. For this purpose, I created the document list below, with the same origins and different destinations. However, this solution would also work for different origins and destinations:

 

 

Based on the Freight Units created, I planned all of them in a single freight order with a linear planning type. Each FU created a new stage in the freight order.

 

Here you can see all the stages created.

 

To demonstrate that our calculation will be based only on the weight of the freight units in our stage, I created a freight agreement with a rate table based on gross weight.

 

At this point I add our carrier. However, my freight order is not enabled to perform the charge calculation.

 

In this configuration example, when updating the FO status to "Ready for transportation", consignment orders are automatically created.

 

The charge calculation is automatically performed, based on the weight of the freight unit stage. If there were more than one FU with the same origin and destination, they would be grouped in the same consignment order.

As I said before, the weight considered in the calculation is only for the delivered FU, and does not contain data from other Freight units.

Note that the stage is based on the FUs and not on the linear stages from freight order. In this way, the FO continues with all the planning capabilities and the CO with the data necessary for calculating CT-e.

After this, the freight settlement is created. In this case, charge distribution was activated in this document. It is possible to see that only the items related to the delivery of the CO stage were considered in the charge distribution. This way, we avoid all the developments normally done in projects in Brazil where we need to follow the planning in a linear manner.

For automatic CO creation, we need to configure a consignment building profile and associate it with the Freight Order configuration. It is even possible to add more split criteria if it is necessary to create more than one CT-e per stage, for example. It is also possible to determine other moments for automatic CO generation, such as using a specific event such as check-in or departure.

SAP S/4HANA Logistics for Brazil #CT-e SAP Transportation Management Localization

entry.title: Unlocking the Power of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in AI: A Game Changer for SAP Customers
entry.content:
The rapid progression in artificial intelligence (AI), especially with large language models (LLMs), is transforming how businesses approach intelligent decision-making. Yet, as promising as these technologies are, challenges such as hallucination—where AI generates incorrect or irrelevant information—can limit their effectiveness in real-world business applications.

One of the key SAP innovations overcoming this issue is Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). By combining LLMs with real-time data retrieval, RAG provides a powerful solution for generating reliable, accurate insights.

In this blog, we’ll dive into RAG, its importance, and how SAP is empowering its customers to make the most of this cutting-edge technology.

What Exactly is Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)?

RAG offers a breakthrough by blending generative AI models with a dynamic data retrieval process. When posed with a query, the AI doesn’t just rely on pre-trained knowledge. Instead, it actively fetches the most relevant, up-to-date information from databases or document repositories, resulting in responses that are both accurate and contextually grounded.

This hybrid approach greatly reduces the risks of hallucinations and ensures that the AI’s outputs are not only trustworthy but also highly applicable to real-time business scenarios.

The Two Pillars of RAG:

  • Retrieval: This component involves the model retrieving the most pertinent data or documents in response to a query.
  • Generation: Using that retrieved information, the model generates a coherent and contextually relevant answer.

By integrating real-time data into the generation process, businesses can ensure that their AI systems provide insightful, accurate, and timely responses.

Why Do We Need RAG?

While LLMs have revolutionized the use of AI for deriving insights, their limitations in practical business applications are well-known:

  • Hallucinations: LLMs may present factually inaccurate or irrelevant responses when detached from current data.
  • Cost of Fine-Tuning: Customizing models with domain-specific knowledge requires significant time, effort, and cost.
  • Stagnant Knowledge: Once trained, LLMs don’t automatically update, limiting their value in fast-paced industries.

By embedding a retrieval mechanism, RAG addresses these limitations. It ensures that AI-generated responses are not only relevant but also backed by the most recent and accurate information available.

Why is RAG Crucial for SAP’s Customers?

Industries such as supply chain management, human resources, and customer relationship management, where SAP operates, thrive on accurate, up-to-date information. Implementing RAG within these sectors offers:

  • Reliable insights: By grounding AI outputs in the latest business data, responses are more trustworthy and useful.
  • Faster response times: RAG enables AI-driven systems to pull relevant information quickly, streamlining business operations.
  • Cost efficiency: Businesses can use RAG with existing data repositories instead of engaging in costly LLM fine-tuning.

SAP’s Approach to RAG

SAP is actively incorporating RAG into its platform, providing users with the tools to build AI systems tailored to their specific needs. Here’s how SAP is enabling this:

  • Centralized RAG Service: SAP’s scalable, domain-agnostic RAG service integrates smoothly with existing user interfaces. This service ensures that AI models leverage the most up-to-date information while also offering transparency by tracing the sources of data used in responses.
  • Vector Search Engine in SAP HANA Cloud: SAP’s advanced vector engine efficiently stores and queries data embeddings, making retrieval faster and more efficient.

Document Grounding in SAP’s Joule and AI Services

SAP’s AI services, including Joule—an AI copilot—are enhancing their capabilities through RAG:

  • Indexing & Retrieval: Documents are transformed into vector embeddings, which are stored and retrieved for accurate, contextual responses.
  • Grounded Generation: The retrieved data informs AI-generated answers, significantly improving accuracy and relevance.

Real-World Impact: RAG in SAP SuccessFactors

One of the most promising applications of RAG within SAP’s ecosystem is in the realm of HR, specifically within SAP SuccessFactors. Typically, answering HR-related queries could take up to 20 minutes per question, putting a strain on HR teams.

With RAG integrated into tools like Joule, employees can now access relevant policies instantly. By querying policy documents directly, the system retrieves relevant sections, generating accurate answers in seconds. This has led to a 35% reduction in the volume of HR inquiries.

Conclusion

The introduction of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is revolutionizing how businesses can harness AI for more accurate, grounded, and reliable insights.

SAP’s integration of this technology into platforms like Joule and BTP allows its customers to take full advantage of AI-driven decision-making without the high costs and complexity of fine-tuning models. As a result, SAP customers are poised to unlock significant value by adopting RAG in their day-to-day operations.

entry.title: SAP CAP UI5 Application
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have created CAP application, in which i am having the srv, db, app folders. I am not using any managed app router. Installed @SAP/approuter inside app/router  folder. Created one UI application inside the app folder.

After deployment UI application is not running. It seems the routing is incorrect. But could not understood what i am missing or what i have to change. 

Below are the details of the applications -

MTA.yaml 

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: config
description: A simple CAP project.
version: 1.0.0
modules:
# CONFIG SERVICE
- name: config-srv
  type: nodejs
  path: gen/srv
  requires:
  - name: config-db
  - name: config-auth
  provides:
  - name: srv-api
    properties:
      srv-url: ${default-url}
  parameters:
    buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
    readiness-health-check-http-endpoint: /health
    readiness-health-check-type: http
  build-parameters:
    builder: npm
# DB DEPLOYER
- name: config-db-deployer
  type: hdb
  path: gen/db
  requires:
  - name: config-db
  parameters:
    buildpack: nodejs_buildpack

#APP ROUTER
- name: config
  type: approuter.nodejs
  path: app/router
  requires:
  - name: srv-api
    group: destinations
    properties:
      forwardAuthToken: true
      name: srv-api
      url: ~{srv-url}
  - name: config-auth
  - name: config_html_repo_runtime
  - name: config-destination-service
    group: destinations
    properties:
      forwardAuthToken: false
      name: ui5
      url: https://ui5.sap.com
  parameters:
    disk-quota: 256M
    keep-existing-routes: true
    memory: 256M

#APP CONTENT
- name: config-app-content
  type: com.sap.application.content
  path: .
  requires:
  - name: config_html_repo_host
    parameters:
      content-target: true
  build-parameters:
    build-result: resources
    requires:
    - artifacts:
      - uiconfig.zip
      name: uiconfig
      target-path: resources/

# HTML5 APP

- name: uiconfig
  type: html5
  path: app/uiconfig
  build-parameters:
    build-result: dist
    builder: custom
    commands:
    - npm install
    - npm run build:cf
    supported-platforms: []

resources:
- name: config-db
  type: com.sap.xs.hdi-container
  parameters:
    service: hana
    service-plan: hdi-shared

- name: config-auth
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    config:
      tenant-mode: dedicated
      xsappname: config-${org}-${space}
    path: ./xs-security.json
    service: xsuaa
    service-plan: application

- name: config_html_repo_host
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: html5-apps-repo
    service-plan: app-host

- name: config_html_repo_runtime
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: html5-apps-repo
    service-plan: app-runtime

- name: config-destination-service
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    config:
      HTML5Runtime_enabled: false
      init_data:
        instance:
          destinations:
          - Authentication: NoAuthentication
            Name: ui5
            ProxyType: Internet
            Type: HTTP
            URL: https://ui5.sap.com
          existing_destinations_policy: update
      version: 1.0.0
    service: destination
    service-name: config-destination-service
    service-plan: lite
parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
  - builder: custom
    commands:
    - npm ci
    - npx cds build --production

Routers 

App-> router-> xs-app.json

{
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/app/(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "localDir": ".",
      "cacheControl": "no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate"
    },
    {
      "source": "^/appconfig/",
      "localDir": ".",
      "cacheControl": "no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate"
    },
    {
      "source": "(.*)",
      "target": "/uiconfig/$1",
      "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt"
    },
    
    {
      "source": "^/(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "destination": "srv-api",
      "csrfProtection": true
    }
  ],
  "welcomeFile": "/uiconfig/"
}

App-> uiconfig-> xs-app.json

{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^/test-resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/test-resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    },

entry.title: BPC EPM SSO
entry.content:
Hi 

We would like to implement EPM Single sign on to our landscape.

We recently updated our password policy to align with our network security standards, which now stipulates that passwords should not expire. However, SAP’s current requirement for regular password changes is causing conflicts with our users, as it enforces expiration outside of our policy.

To address this, we would like to implement Single Sign-On (SSO) for seamless access. While we understand that users can access EPM via the web, the web login still requires password changes as per SAP’s policy. Our goal is to enable users to connect to SAP and EPM without being prompted for passwords, leveraging our existing network security credentials.

Could you please provide guidance on how we can achieve this integration?”

kindly let us know any more requirements from our end.

Thanks & Regards

entry.title: Re: How to Gather Error Logs in sap CPI.
entry.content:
Hi @Akhil0434 

sounds like you need to handle errors and send consolidated error reports via email.

Here are a few suggestions to help you achieve this:

  1. Use a Data Store: Instead of sending an email for each error immediately, you can store the error details in a Data Store. Once all records are processed, you can retrieve the stored errors and send a single email with all the error details. As suggested by @manoj_khavatkopp A Good and Clean approach!!

  2. Exception Subprocess with Aggregator: Although you mentioned that the Gather step isn’t working in the exception subprocess, you might want to try using an Aggregator pattern. This can help you collect all error messages and then process them together.

  3. Of Course Custom Script 😅: You can use a Groovy script to collect error messages. Store each error in a list and then, at the end of the process, compile these errors into a single email.

Here’s a rough outline of how you might implement the Data Store approach: I too would have done the same in your shoes!!

Step-by-Step Guide

  1. Create Data Store:

    • In your SAP CPI tenant, create a Data Store to hold error messages. You can name it something like “ErrorDataStore”.

  2. Store Errors:

    • In your exception subprocess, add a step to store each error message in the Data Store. Use a unique key for each entry to avoid overwriting.

  3. End of Processing:

    • After all records are processed, add a step to retrieve all error messages from the Data Store.

  4. Compile Errors:

    • Combine all retrieved error messages into a single string or document.

  5. Send Email:

    • Use the compiled error report to send a single email with all the error details.

Hope this helps!! Ciao! Cloud Integration

entry.title: Re: How to create hovering effect in SAP Build apps

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI - Encryption and Decryption using PGP keys
entry.content:
Please NOTE: This is not the official PGP key creation recommendation. Please refer to the CPI help pages as well.
Generating PGP keys using Kleopatra tooling is fine, but you need to be aware that SAP cannot give any support on this tool, so you need to get familiar with this tool on your own and need to know what you are doing.
Please especially consider compatibility constraints as CPI nowadays is only supporting PGP standard according to RFC4880. In case the tooling defaults to options introduced with the newer standard (ietf.org/rfc/rfc9580.txt) make sure you are using appropriate compatibility switches offered by Kleopatra tooling. Please also refer to KBA about solving related issues later in IFlow processing: 3525769 - PGP Decryptor Step Fails With an "Unknown Packet Tag" error in Cloud Integration - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP UI5 Application
entry.content:
Hi @DebashishDas,

I think there are a couple of things wrong/weird about your project.

  1. First of all, when routing to the uiconfig application in the xs-app.json of the approuter, you want to use the ID of the UI5 app as the target (see its manifest.json), instead of the path to it.
  2. Also, please keep in mind that the routes are being checked by the approuter from top to bottom. This means that your uiconfig route ((.*)) will prevent all other routes for below from every being accessed. This is true for both of your xs-app.json files.

For an extensive sample of how to deploy a UI5 app to the html5-apps-repo using the mta approach, see this: https://github.com/SAP-samples/multi-cloud-html5-apps-samples/tree/main/standalone-approuter-html5-runtime-mta-hello-world

Best, Nico

entry.title: Request for Guidance on Variable Pay setup integrated with EC – Seeking Expert Advice
entry.content:
Hello Compensation Gurus,

I hope you're all doing well! I’ve been following your insightful posts for quite some time and have gained a wealth of knowledge from your contributions on Compensation & Variable Pay. I truly appreciate the value this community provides, and I’m reaching out today for some expert advice.

I am an EC consultant, and I’ve recently started working on a project involving Variable Pay. Since this is my first hands-on experience in this area, I have a couple of questions and would be really grateful for your insights.

  1. Bonus Calculation for Different Employee Populations
    The client I’m working with has two employee groups: one hourly-paid and the other bi-weekly salaried. For the salaried population, the client wants to use the annual salary for bonus calculation. For the hourly population, they’d like to use a custom field to import a reference salary in EC, which would then be used for bonus calculation.
    Is it possible to achieve this in a single template where different salary data is used for these two groups? If so, I’d really appreciate any tips or best practices on how to implement this effectively.

  2. Excluding Specific Leave Types from Bonus Calculation
    The client has several leave event reasons, including Long-Term Disability (LTD) and Short-Term Disability (STD). They want to exclude LTD from the bonus calculation, but consider STD leave for up to 183 days for bonus eligibility.
    Can this be handled via proration, or would another custom method (e.g., using a field to track eligibility) be more appropriate? Any advice on how to best approach this would be greatly appreciated.

I understand you all must be busy, but as I’m still building my hands-on experience with Variable Pay, I’d be extremely grateful for any guidance or suggestions you can share.

Thank you in advance for your time and assistance. Looking forward to learning from your collective expertise!

Best Regards

entry.title: Executing desktop automation for multiple users
entry.content:
Hi,

Customer wants to deploy some standard automations - eg: 4FP (Post goods movement) for S/4Hana Cloud. This automation expects data to be maintained in excel sheet on the user's system and a trigger scheduled along with desktop agent configured to pick and execute the automation. The file location and communication user/password for S4HC are entered while deploying the project,

For such automations, if there are multiple users who would want to use this automation, what is the technical approach to be given to users?

1. We cannot have a common trigger to execute for multiple users. So, should each user have a project deployed on his/her name and a trigger per project?

2. I don't think it's possible, but can we have multiple versions of the same project and for each version,  a trigger?

3. Can these automations read a file from shared folder/drive/sharepoint or does it mean, we need to customize the automation?

entry.title: FI substitution not triggered from SD Invoice
entry.content:
We currently have an issue with FI substitutions when creating the FI document out of a SD invoice.

Prerequisite: BSEG-MWSKZ = 'L1' AND BSEG-KUNNR IN ZFI_0002_Z4_KUNNR

Substitution (if prerequisite is met): SCB Ind. Constant value 566

When creating an invoice in FI the substitution is triggered and the SCB Indicator is filled but when we create an SD Order and creating an invoice (with all the requirements met) the SCB Indicator is not filled.

Do you have any hints or clues on how we can solve this issue?

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports 2020 export to odbc
entry.content:
[Just upgraded to CR 2020.]

When loading a report that uses data from a previously exported Excel ODBC in CR2016, I get the error: "Failed to load database information. Details: Access/Excel(DAO) connection is no longer supported, use another alternative if possible, e.g. ODBC/JDBC." Therefore, I think exporting to ODBC Excel/Access is no longer a viable solution in CR 2020.

This is unfortunate because I need to access the same Oracle database with two different logins for data for one report. CR [or Oracle setup] doesn't seem to allow this. I have tried a number of different methods to accomplish it without success. So, I have had to retrieve data into Report1, move the previous data in excel by clearing the excel Name Manager info, change the excel tab name, then re-export the new CR data to the same Excel ODBC file, then use it as a secondary data source for Report2 without needing to modify this Report2. [It was a little clunky, but got the job done.]

For the new CR2020 install, I have installed the MSOffice 365 Database Engine [and 2016] and various items. The "Export" to ODBC Excel/Access does not seem to work, regardless of AccessEngine install. Attempting to get it to work in another way, I have successfully connected to the ODBC file using the MS ODBC configuration settings [start menu ODBC, each file needs a new connection in Drive C:, then create a New Connection using ODBC (RDO) in CR2020]. I currently can only retrieve data from the ODBC file. I am not sure how to update data in the ODBC file using this setup. I may need to incorporate SQL Update commands in Report1 to update the external Excel ODBC file. Then use that ODBC connection as secondary input data in Report2.

Another possiblity would be to just use the CR2020 excel output file as a datasource without the ODBC connection. I have not tried this before, but will attempt this in the near future. Then, every time the excel is output from Report1 and overwritten, the data is updated for Report2.

Any other work arounds or ideas are appreciated.
